_doc: TTL table for the Exterminator Sentinel Stage 1 stale-feed check (check 1).
Every feed MUST declare where its TTL came from. Two honest states:
ttl_verified=true   ttl_source cites the publisher/artifact that defines it.
Breaching fail_after_seconds may raise status=fail.
ttl_verified=false  no authoritative TTL exists in code or artifact.
ttl_seconds is an OPERATOR-PROPOSED value awaiting
verification; the check is CAPPED AT warn and the
evidence carries ttl_source=operator_verify. A sentinel
must never fail a gate on a number nobody ratified.
warn_after_seconds  = the declared TTL (contract breach).
fail_after_seconds  = operational floor above which staleness is not a
scheduling jitter but an outage. null => never fail.
Reading order for ttl_seconds at runtime: the artifact's own ttl_seconds
field WINS when present, so this table can never silently drift from the
publisher. The value here is the fallback + the citation of record.

var_state.ttl_source: artifact:ttl_seconds; publisher pins var_state.v1 at chad/risk/portfolio_var.py:388

positions_snapshot._note: This is the INDEPENDENT broker leg used by check 4. If it goes stale, check 4 loses its only independent truth source and degrades to warn (blind), never to a false ok.

kraken_ticks._note: Crypto ticks are 24/7, so a gap is never a market-hours artifact. The 30s TTL is a real-time contract; fail_after is set to 600s so ordinary 5-min scan jitter cannot manufacture a fail.

bars._note: ibkr_bars_cache.json declares NO ttl_seconds and no publisher pins one. 900 is a proposal, not a contract. Capped at warn until an operator ratifies a TTL. Related known-broken units: chad-ibkr-daily-bars-refresh (exit 1), chad-ibkr-bar-provider.

exit_overlay_heartbeat._note: fail_after = ttl*2, matching health rule R14's STALE threshold at chad/ops/health_monitor_rules.py:1134. This feed additionally gets the R14 BLIND cross-check (see check 1 blind_check).

equity_history._note: Appended daily by chad/ops/equity_history_publisher.py; no TTL is declared anywhere. 86400 reflects the documented daily cadence and 129600 (36h) allows one missed publish before warning. Capped at warn until ratified.

drawdown_state._note: W3B-1. Written by chad-drawdown-publisher.timer every 120s (installed unit; repo copies under ops/systemd/). fail_after=3x TTL. The P1-3 'orphaned publisher' era (10-day-stale file, closed 2026-06-17) is exactly the outage this row exists to page on -- until W3B-1 neither freshness net watched this artifact.

ibkr_watchdog_last.ttl_source: artifact:ttl_seconds (pass-through of runtime/ibkr_status.json ttl, stamped at chad/core/ibkr_watchdog.py:388); output cadence contract = 120s installed chad-ibkr-watchdog.timer
ibkr_watchdog_last._note: W3B-1. ts_unix is a NUMERIC epoch -- _parse_ts numeric support added in the same commit. warn_after=3x cadence mirrors the watchdog's own internal 3x-ttl staleness convention (chad/core/ibkr_watchdog.py:243-246); a bare 120s warn would flap against the 120s timer + RandomizedDelaySec=15s on a 5-min sentinel scan. The artifact is a self-heartbeat (zero code readers); its value is proving the IBKR-health alerter itself is alive -- the P1-4 44-day silent death is the target.

_doc: R14 blind pattern (chad/ops/health_monitor_rules.py:1134, commit 75878b2).
A fresh heartbeat reporting evaluated=0 does NOT prove health -- it may be
watching nothing. Blindness is only provable against a SEPARATELY PUBLISHED
truth artifact that is itself proven fresh. If that artifact is stale or
absent, we stay silent rather than cry wolf.

allowlist.reason: Staged deletions of _archive/bak_purge_* and _archive/bak_quarantine_* backups, present in the working tree since before this sentinel existed. Documented allowlist entry pending disposition -- REMOVE this entry once the deletions are committed or reverted.

_doc: Enumerated from docs/schemas/ + code pins. Only contracts with a pinned
schema_version string are ENFORCED. Contracts known to be unpinned are
listed under unpinned_known so the check reports them honestly as a
pre-existing gap (warn) instead of pretending they pass.

runtime/position_guard_drift.json._note: Consumer ops/live_readiness_publish.py:195 accepts v1|v2|v3, so this check must too.

unpinned_known.runtime/reconciliation_state.json: No schema_version pinned by any publisher (ops/reconcile_positions.py / chad/ops/reconciliation_publisher.py). Pre-existing gap.
unpinned_known.runtime/positions_snapshot.json: No schema_version; publisher chad/portfolio/ibkr_portfolio_collector_v2.py emits source/ts_utc/ttl_seconds only.
unpinned_known.runtime/profit_lock_state.json: No schema_version for THIS payload (chad/risk/profit_lock.py:842 pins pnl_state.v1, a different artifact).

_doc: Check 2 + check 3 store policy.
PERMITTED stores legitimately CONTAIN untrusted/placeholder rows -- that is
their job. The evidence writer is the PRODUCER of untrusted rows and the
ledger is hash-chained, so rows are marked, never deleted. Treating
presence-in-FILLS as a leak is wrong.
SCORED stores feed SCR effective_trades and MUST be free of them.
fills._placeholder_note: Do NOT test top-level fill_price == 100.0. chad/execution/paper_exec_evidence_writer.py:1952 deliberately ZEROES the top-level numeric fields on a caught placeholder and preserves the original under extra.placeholder_fill_price. A caught placeholder therefore never has fill_price==100.0. Detect via extra.placeholder_fill_price / extra.trust_state==PLACEHOLDER / the 'placeholder' tag, then assert CONTAINMENT (status=rejected or pnl_untrusted). An UNCONTAINED placeholder means the defense at :1923 failed.

_doc: Check 8. There is NO production veto-rate baseline anywhere in the repo or
runtime -- veto decisions leave only log lines (ML_SHADOW / ML_VETO), no
durable artifact, no counter. val_veto_rate_at_0.65 in the manifest is a
TRAINING-time validation statistic, not a production baseline. Comparing a
live rate against it would be a category error, so check 8 emits
warn 'no_baseline' honestly until a real baseline is published.

services._doc: Check 5 reads systemd state with a read-only query only. No start/stop/reset/enable is ever issued -- locked by the anti-auto-healing tests.
